signal_title: Online Safety (Designated Internet Services—Class 1A and Class 1B Material) Industry Standard 2024
risk_rationale: The new Australian legislation mandates safety standards to prevent access to unsuitable content for children, which may require immediate product changes to ensure compliance with local regulations.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: No mature content surfaced to minors
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Mandatory safety standards for internet services to prevent access to unsuitable content for children
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline requires no mature content to be surfaced to minors, but the new legislation may require additional safety measures or standards to be implemented.

remediations.title: Enhance Content Filtering Mechanisms
remediations.description: Implement additional content filtering mechanisms to comply with the new Australian safety standards, ensuring that all unsuitable content is effectively blocked for minors.
acceptance_criteria: All content flagged as unsuitable is blocked for users under 18 in Australia
Regular audits and updates to filtering mechanisms to ensure compliance

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new legislation could result in fines or restrictions on operations in Australia, impacting user trust and market presence.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed gap analysis to identify specific content categories considered unsuitable under the new legislation.
Engage with Australian regulatory bodies to clarify compliance expectations and timelines.
Develop and deploy enhanced content filtering solutions tailored to the Australian market.
